pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies London, UK.
Task description states August 31, 1888. Corresponds to the real historical date of the first canonical Jack the Ripper murder (Mary Ann Nichols).
Calculated using astropy.time.Time for 1888-08-31T03:00:00 UTC. The claimed JD 2410884.625 is off by exactly 4 days.
Matched with explicit instructions in the task description and config.ini parsing keys in export_result.sh.
Task description requests saving notes to ~/Desktop/whitechapel_moon.txt, which resolves to this path for the 'ga' user and is confirmed by export_result.sh.
Task description requests saving to ~/Pictures/stellarium/, confirmed by export_result.sh which scans this directory.

privileged_info_summary: The task accurately requires investigating the sky over London (51.5 N, -0.1 W, 0.8988 rad, -0.0017 rad) for the historical date of August 31, 1888 at 03:00 UTC, which corresponds to the first canonical Jack the Ripper murder. Export and prompt paths for notes and screenshots exactly match the metadata. Required Stellarium display flags (atmosphere, landscape, cardinal points, azimuthal grid) correspond directly to the prompt instructions. However, the claimed approximate Julian Date of 2410884.625 is incorrect by 4 days; astropy calculation confirms the true Julian Date for this time is 2410880.625.
